

::: callout-tip
## Proportionality and constraints

Apply open and reproducible practice **proportionately**, but **always** make constraints explicit.

- Constraints can include: sensitive/personal data, contractual/IP limits, governance restrictions, time/resource limits.
- “Proportionate” means choosing a feasible approach and documenting it (e.g., in README, DMP, ethics/governance notes).
- Even when outputs cannot be open, they should still be **well described** (metadata, documentation, access route).
:::
